description: Mack and Elena reflect on Talbot's motives and history, trying to anticipate his next move. Mack realizes Talbot might seek out his family to prove himself as a hero, and he resolves that if a lethal response is required, he should be the one to take the shot. Their discussion underscores the moral weight of the choices ahead.

transcript.line: We can forget about finding the Quinjet.
Then how do we find him?
I don't know. We have to think like him. He's a man we all knew, a man that had a lot of stars and stripes on his uniform. What does he want to do now that he has all this power?
Death and destruction. That's what they all want.
Ah, I'm not so sure. He risked his life to save us from the Remorath.
And then he went on a killing spree. He's in full-villain mode.
Yeah, but, everyone's the hero in their own story.
Ah, I mean it. Did he say anything before he went into the machine?
He kept saying, “I can fix this.”
I don't know. His mistakes, his reputation? Maybe he's trying to prove that he's still a good soldier, a good man.
